income_thresholds.description: Employees earning up to Rs 21,000 per month (gross salary) are covered under ESI. For disabled employees, the threshold is Rs 25,000 per month.

exclusion_rules.description: Employees earning more than Rs 21,000/month gross salary are not covered (Rs 25,000 for disabled employees)
ESI applies only to establishments with 10 or more employees (20 or more in some states like Maharashtra and Chandigarh). Below this threshold, the employer is not required to register.
The employer MUST be registered with ESIC. If the employer is not registered (violation of law), the employee cannot access ESI benefits directly.
Self-employed individuals, freelancers, and gig workers are not covered under ESI

family_criteria.family_definition: Employee plus dependents: spouse, children (all ages), dependent parents. All dependents are covered for medical benefits at ESI hospitals and dispensaries.

covered_procedures: COMPREHENSIVE medical care at ESI hospitals and dispensaries
Maternity benefits: 26 weeks paid leave + medical expenses
Sickness benefit: 70% of wages for up to 91 days per year
Extended sickness benefit: for 34 specified long-term diseases (up to 2 years at 80% wages)
Temporary disability benefit: 90% of wages during work injury period
Permanent disability benefit: monthly pension proportional to disability %
Dependent benefit: 90% of wages as pension to dependents if worker dies from work injury
Confinement expenses: if delivery at non-ESI hospital
Super specialty referral through tie-up hospitals
excluded_procedures: Fertility treatment and IVF (not standard coverage)
Treatment at non-empanelled private hospitals (except emergencies)
Ayurveda, Homeopathy (available at select ESI centers only)

required_documents.name: ESI Registration Number (from employer)
required_documents.notes: Employer registers the establishment and all eligible employees with ESIC. Employee receives an ESI number.

required_documents.name: Salary Slip / Wage Certificate
required_documents.notes: To verify that gross monthly salary is within the Rs 21,000 threshold

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: For e-KYC and biometric authentication at ESI hospitals

required_documents.name: Passport-size Photographs
required_documents.notes: 2 photos each for employee and each dependent for ESI card

required_documents.name: Family Details and Dependent Proof
required_documents.notes: Marriage certificate, children birth certificates, parent dependency declaration

required_documents.name: Bank Account Details
required_documents.notes: For cash benefit disbursement (sickness benefit, maternity benefit, disability pension)

enrollment_channels: Employer (mandatory enrollment by employer for eligible employees)
ESIC portal (esic.gov.in) for self-registration by employer
ESI hospital or dispensary (for Pehchan card issuance)
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Employer registers the establishment on ESIC portal (esic.gov.in) using the Employer Registration Form
Step 2: Employer adds all eligible employees (salary up to Rs 21,000/month) in the ESIC system
Step 3: Each employee is assigned a unique ESI Insurance Number (17-digit)
Step 4: Employee submits Aadhaar, photographs, and family/dependent details to employer
Step 5: Employer uploads employee and dependent details on ESIC portal
Step 6: ESI Pehchan Card (biometric smart card) is issued to employee and dependents
Step 7: Monthly contribution is deducted: 0.75% from employee salary + 3.25% by employer
Step 8: Employee and dependents can visit any ESI hospital or dispensary with Pehchan Card for treatment
Step 9: For super-specialty treatment, ESI hospital refers to empanelled tertiary hospitals
description_for_embedding: Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) provides COMPREHENSIVE social security to organized sector employees earning up to Rs 21,000/month. Unlike pure health insurance schemes, ESI covers medical care, sickness benefit (70% wages for 91 days), maternity benefit (26 weeks paid leave), temporary and permanent disability pension, dependent pension on work-related death, and funeral expenses. Employer contributes 3.25% and employee 0.75% of salary. Coverage extends to employee and all dependents (spouse, children, parents). Applies to establishments with 10+ employees (20+ in some states). Treatment at dedicated ESI hospitals and dispensaries network. Enrollment is done by the employer, not the individual.
